steps.title: Set up cases for the absolute value
steps.content:
The equation $2|6x - 1| = 6x + 9$ requires removing the absolute value bars. This creates two cases based on the sign of $6x - 1$:

Case 1: If $6x - 1 \geq 0$, then $|6x - 1| = 6x - 1$
Case 2: If $6x - 1 < 0$, then $|6x - 1| = -(6x - 1)$

steps.title: Solve Case 1
steps.content:
When $6x - 1 \geq 0$:
$$2(6x - 1) = 6x + 9$$
$$12x - 2 = 6x + 9$$
$$6x = 11$$
$$x = \frac{11}{6}$$

Check validity: $6 \cdot \frac{11}{6} - 1 = 11 - 1 = 10 > 0$ ✓ This solution is valid.

steps.title: Solve Case 2
steps.content:
When $6x - 1 < 0$:
$$2(-(6x - 1)) = 6x + 9$$
$$-12x + 2 = 6x + 9$$
$$-18x = 7$$
$$x = -\frac{7}{18}$$

Check validity: $6 \cdot \left(-\frac{7}{18}\right) - 1 = -\frac{7}{3} - 1 = -\frac{10}{3} < 0$ ✓ This solution is valid.

steps.title: Add the two solutions
steps.content:
Sum the solutions found:
$$\frac{11}{6} + \left(-\frac{7}{18}\right) = \frac{11}{6} - \frac{7}{18}$$

Find common denominator (18):
$$\frac{33}{18} - \frac{7}{18} = \frac{26}{18} = \frac{13}{9}$$
